created_from: User prompt: Can a conformal U(1) BSM extension with a dark scalar, a dark gauge bosons and a dark fermion explain the NANOGrav signal with a cosmological FOPT?
commands: python -c '<AST expression validation for JSON handoff expressions>'
warnings: No existing output directory was present, so local novelty was checked only against the absence of existing local handoff files.
No numerical FOPT or PTA result is claimed by this proposer handoff.
approximations: Higgs portal coupling is fixed to zero for the FOPT backend parameterization.
Gauge kinetic mixing with hypercharge is fixed to zero for the FOPT backend parameterization.
The semianalytic high-temperature/small-field expansion backend is assumed to be the intended deterministic FOPT implementation.

gauge_groups.name: SU(3)_c
gauge_groups.notes: All new dark-sector fields are singlets.

gauge_groups.name: SU(2)_L
gauge_groups.notes: All new dark-sector fields are singlets.

gauge_groups.name: U(1)_Y
gauge_groups.notes: All new dark-sector fields have zero hypercharge.

gauge_groups.name: U(1)_D
gauge_groups.notes: Broken by the Coleman-Weinberg vev of the complex dark scalar Phi.

global_symmetries.name: classical_scale_invariance
global_symmetries.notes: Forbids explicit scalar mass terms and dimensionful dark-sector parameters before dimensional transmutation.

global_symmetries.name: accidental_dark_fermion_number_or_residual_parity
global_symmetries.notes: May stabilize the dark fermion when no additional dark-sector operators are introduced; not required by the FOPT backend.

particle_content.name: Phi
particle_content.role: Coleman-Weinberg dark scalar and FOPT order parameter

particle_content.name: X_mu
particle_content.role: Massive dark photon after U(1)_D breaking; dominant bosonic CW and thermal-barrier contributor

particle_content.name: psi_plus
particle_content.role: Dark fermion Weyl component with positive U(1)_D charge

particle_content.name: psi_minus
particle_content.role: Dark fermion Weyl component with negative U(1)_D charge; cancels U(1)_D gauge and gravitational anomalies with psi_plus

lagrangian_terms.name: dark_scalar_kinetic
lagrangian_terms.notes: D_mu Phi = (partial_mu - I*2*g_D*X_mu) Phi for q_Phi = 2.

lagrangian_terms.name: dark_fermion_kinetic
lagrangian_terms.notes: Covariant derivatives use q_psi_plus = 1 and q_psi_minus = -1.

lagrangian_terms.name: classically_scale_invariant_potential
lagrangian_terms.notes: No explicit mass term; lambda_D is CW-dependent and is not scanned in the semianalytic backend.

lagrangian_terms.name: higgs_portal_fixed_zero
lagrangian_terms.notes: Allowed by symmetries but fixed to lambda_HP = 0 for the proposed backend-compatible FOPT parameterization.

lagrangian_terms.name: kinetic_mixing_fixed_zero
lagrangian_terms.notes: Allowed for two U(1) gauge groups but fixed to epsilon = 0 for the proposed backend-compatible FOPT parameterization.

free_parameters.name: v_D
free_parameters.range: positive real, GeV; downstream scan should choose PTA-relevant values

free_parameters.name: y_D
free_parameters.range: nonnegative perturbative real coupling with beta_lambda > 0

beta_lambda.description: Coleman-Weinberg beta coefficient in the backend mass-over-vev basis.

m_chi_squared.description: CW pseudo-dilaton radial scalar mass squared.

lambda_D.determination: Coleman-Weinberg minimization condition; renormalization-scale dependent and not scanned by semianalytic_pipeline

lambda_HP.description: Higgs portal fixed to zero for backend-compatible dark-sector FOPT scan.

epsilon.description: Hypercharge-dark kinetic mixing fixed to zero for backend-compatible FOPT scan.

q_psi_plus.description: Positive dark Weyl fermion U(1)_D charge convention.

q_psi_minus.description: Negative dark Weyl fermion U(1)_D charge convention.

assumptions: The dark sector is decoupled from the Standard Model for the FOPT backend scan.
The model is classically scale invariant at tree level.
The FOPT is controlled by the single radial background chi.
The Coleman-Weinberg quartic is dependent and not a scan parameter.
The critic-agent should verify the stated anomaly cancellation and charge conventions before downstream use.

backend_compatibility.notes: The model is a conformal single-field Coleman-Weinberg setup with masses proportional to chi and no explicit scalar mass term.

ssb_pattern.notes: The dark photon becomes massive and the radial scalar is a CW pseudo-dilaton.

vev_convention.notes: Unitary gauge is implied for the broken U(1)_D phase.

tree_level_potential.name: conformal_quartic
tree_level_potential.notes: lambda_D is dependent through the CW minimization condition; no chi**2 mass term is present.

field_dependent_masses.notes: From |D_mu Phi|**2 with Phi = chi/sqrt(2) and q_Phi = 2 under the convention where m_X = 2*g_D*chi.
From the Yukawa operators -(y_D/2) Phi_dagger psi_plus psi_plus - (y_D/2) Phi psi_minus psi_minus + h.c. with Phi = chi/sqrt(2).
CW pseudo-dilaton mass at the broken minimum; not double-counted in beta_lambda for backend scans.

thermal_masses.notes: Representative Abelian Debye contribution from one charged complex scalar with q=2 and two Weyl fermions with charges +1 and -1; downstream FOPT implementation may use its own backend convention.
Analytic scalar thermal-mass placeholder for validation; semianalytic_pipeline primarily consumes gb/gf/dof and CW relations.

expression_symbols.meaning: Real radial dark scalar background field
CW beta coefficient for the quartic
